summary: Iter-103 bundle (7 items) — 9/9 backend pytest passed, frontend visual + integration verified. (1) Score cap: matching.score_therapist returns total<=95.0 on synthetic perfect-pair, -1 sentinel preserved on filter; admin/requests detail scan shows zero match-scores >95. (2) Email templates: GET /api/admin/email-templates lists both 'new_referral_inquiry' and 'prelaunch_invite' (>=14 templates), PUT /admin/email-templates/new_referral_inquiry persists subject. (3) Hard-capacity: disabled.client_type=['family','group'], disabled.age_group=['child']; counts.client_type and counts.age_group populated; protections array contains client_type and age_group entries. (4) outreach_agent._NRI_OVERRIDES_CACHE + _load_template_overrides exist and importable. (5) RequestsPanel: f6304cc8 row renders red AlertTriangle + 5/30 in red — request-low-coverage-f6304cc8-7b8f-49cc-9572-70c85dcc394a found visible (also 0040edfc and 7c9d60e4). (6) IntakeUI WhoStep: client-type-family-disabled, client-type-group-disabled, age-group-child-disabled all present with cursor-not-allowed + line-through + opacity-70 classes; visible struck-through in screenshot. (7) Admin More menu: Matching simulator + Settings + Coverage gaps all load via useAdminClient. Coverage gaps panel renders Protected HARD selections including the new client_type (family 28/30, group 11/30) and age_group (child 5/30) sections — perfect end-to-end. PatientResults Insurance fix verified at code level: pages/PatientResults.jsx:342 IIFE reads insurance_name regardless of payment_type (gated UI prevented direct auth'd test). No match scores >95% on dashboard or admin pages.

issues: Spec mentions data-testid 'client-type-warning' and 'age-group-warning' — these were NOT found on the WhoStep when family/group/child are disabled. Either the warning UI fires only on click-attempt, or testids are missing. If a static warning hint should always appear when disabled pills exist, those testids should be added; otherwise spec wording is misleading.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Confirm whether 'client-type-warning' / 'age-group-warning' testids are intended to render statically below the disabled pills or only on click-attempt. Currently neither testid is present when WhoStep first loads with family/group/child disabled.

OPTIONAL: PatientResults is auth-gated (6-digit code) — couldn't validate the Insurance render via UI for f6304cc8. Code review confirms fix is correct (insurances IIFE in PatientResults.jsx:342 reads insurance_name regardless of payment_type). No code change needed; just flagging that direct UI verification needs a passwordless magic-code flow or test-mode auth bypass.
critical_code_review_comments: matching.py:910 — score cap `if isinstance(total,(int,float)) and total>0: total=min(95.0,total)` is correct: -1 sentinels (filtered) skip the cap because they're not >0. Lossless.

matching.py axis weights audit (per user's triple-check): _score_payment_alignment returns the cash-rate fit slot under the 'payment_alignment' axis (line 417+) — the iter104 'Cash rate above comfortable' display label matches the `payment_alignment` raw key. Not regressed. _urgency_pass uses the `accepted = ladder.get(urgency, set())` lookup with 'asap' branching to {'asap'} only (iter105 fix is intact — `accepted` is a set, not a list).

hard_capacity.py:118-130 client_type and age_group counters use `t.get('client_types') or []` — correct coalescing. The disabled-axis emission uses `< 30` threshold consistent with TARGET_NOTIFIED on the frontend. Symmetric.

outreach_agent.py:48 _load_template_overrides reads from a 'email_template_overrides' collection and stores in module-level cache. The cache is populated via run_outreach_for_request (per spec); since this is a module-level singleton, concurrent calls during a tight async loop are safe (single-writer). Consider adding a TTL if templates change mid-run, but not critical.
AdminDashboard still threads a legacy `client` prop alongside <AdminClientProvider>. Minor wasted work — same critique as iter102. Not new but worth tracking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 3 panels (Simulator/Settings/Coverage gaps) render via useAdminClient; low-coverage badge visible for 3 under-covered requests; intake disabled pills correctly styled. PatientResults insurance fix verified at code level only (UI gated by patient sign-in).

test_credentials: admin: admin123! via sessionStorage.setItem('tv_admin_pwd','admin123!') + nav to /admin/dashboard. Patient PatientResults page is gated behind /sign-in 6-digit Resend code — no auto-bypass available, code-level inspection used instead.
seed_data_creation: None. Test PUT on new_referral_inquiry restored to original after persistence verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration103_bundle.py — 9 tests covering score cap (2), email templates module (1), hard_capacity module direct call (1), outreach agent imports (1), and HTTP integration tests (4). NOTE on score_therapist signature: it's score_therapist(t, r) — therapist FIRST, request SECOND. Don't swap. PatientResults at /results/{id} requires patient sign-in (Resend 6-digit code) — can't test directly in preview env without seeded patient session token; recommend adding a debug bypass or a deterministic test patient session for next iteration. The 'client-type-warning' / 'age-group-warning' testids in spec might just be the hint copy that fires on click — current page load shows neither.
rca of the issue: All 7 ship items validated. Notable absence: client-type-warning and age-group-warning testids were not on the WhoStep page-load DOM. May be intentional (only render after a click on a disabled pill) but the spec lists them as expected testids, so worth confirming with the main agent. PatientResults gating prevented live UI verification of the insurance bugfix — code-level inspection of pages/PatientResults.jsx:342 confirms the IIFE returns request.insurance_name unconditionally, which is the correct fix.
